chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, LARKANA C. P. No.S-64 of 2025 Date of Hearing ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 24.03.2025. 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of Main Case. Mr. Ashfaque Hussain Abro, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Muhammad Afzal Jagirani, advocate for respondents No.1 to 4. Mr. Abdul Waris Bhutto, Asstt: A.G, along with SIP Sikander Ali Chandio, PC Asif Ali, HC Manzoor Ahmed and PC Niaz Ali. --------------------- Official respondents No.1 to 5 file statements through learned Assistant A.G which are taken on record. They have denied allegations levelled in the petition. They state that there are number of cases pending against family members of petitioner and there is one FIR No.23/2025 for offence under Section 324, 353 PPC lodged against the petitioner. Learned counsel for the petitioner states that the petitioner is on bail in the aforementioned F.I.R, as for as other FIRs are concerned, they are not against him. He states that he obtained bail and he is ready to face trial but the official respondents are causing harassment to the petitioner and not providing protection in accordance with law. Learned Assistant A.G states that there are number of cases pending against the family members of the petitioner. An FIR has also been lodged against petitioner and in order to save himself, he has filed this petition, he however, submits that official respondents will act in accordance with law. In view of above, official respondents are hereby directed to act in accordance with law and not to cause any harassment to the petitioner, however, it is made clear that the case pending against the petitioner will be decided in accordance with law.

An FIR has also been lodged against petitioner and in order to save himself, he has filed this petition, he however, submits that official respondents will act in accordance with law. In view of above, official respondents are hereby directed to act in accordance with law and not to cause any harassment to the petitioner, however, it is made clear that the case pending against the petitioner will be decided in accordance with law. Copy of this order be sent to the learned Assistant A.G and the respondent No.3 for compliance. The petition is disposed of in above terms. JUDGE
